reviews.review_title: As near to being in space as i ever felt
reviews.review_description: If you ever want to goto space but cant afford it this is a pretty good substitute. The station is quite detailed with info videos to explain things and you get to use the Canadarm and go outside the station - it's like a hands on self guided tour i learnt a lot about the ISS. Being there in VR is like a first hand experience.

Though the recreation of the USOS of the ISS is by all means impressive, I was rather saddened to see that the Russian Orbital Section was walled off and completely inaccessible. Are there any plans to expand the model used in this game to the full interior of the space station? The activities provided are interesting, though only three of them are interactive, and I feel that a few more could be made with the ROS, maybe like a station boost activity or a Progress docking simulator. As it stands currently, only about half of the ISS is currently explorable.

I will however say that the zero-g movement in this game is perfect, using handrails to move around is shocking natural, even if it is weird to experience gravity when what you’re seeing clearly shows that you shouldn’t’ be.

I got this game and I played for 90 minutes straight! This was an amazing experience! I still haven’t finished the game! There are a bunch of videos that you can find while exploring from THE REAL ISS! The achievements are fun to collect. You even have a Mission Control helping you out as you repair things. The space walk is a heck of a time, and you can go in any angle you want, you can even do flips in this game! The training is very fun, and I have yet to complete the “professional astronaut” achievement! If you want a fun, exciting, educational space game to play, MISSION ISS IS FOR YOU!

I love this game. I'm a huge human spaceflight fan, and it's sometimes hard to grasp what it's like being on the ISS from earth. This gives you a nice three-dimensional view into what it's like to live and work in space. While the graphics are not stellar, it is able to recreate the mechanical environment well. The motion is fluid and you actually feel like you're floating through the ISS. And the videos are a nice touch, always great to see some familiar faces up there. The game itself is short, and could benefit from another mission or two. But I find myself coming back time and time again to imagine myself as an astronaut. I've played a lot of space games in my day, and this one is a winner. If you're looking for an authentic ISS experience, look no further.
I mean it kinda almost made me throw up

What I was really missing is the motion of the station flying over the planet at 8 km/sec. It is stuck over the north Italy ! This is hardly educational.
The second unexpected fact is the whole Russian part of the station, which supplies the life support and was the core of the station at the beginning is not even mentioned. You can see it only during the spacewalk, which is an amazing experience.
My son realized the Canada Arm is equipped at both ends with identical attachments. There are several mounting points around the station so that it can move where needed. That's cool.

The whole experience is pretty cool, but from what I see - only the international part of the station has the interior modelled. You can't get into the Russian side - the hatch is just closed. You also can't enter any docked vehicles nor see the Bigelow module. The Earth also doesn't seem to be moving underneath and the orbit is not progressing. Feels like half of what it could be, thus the lowered rating.
